description: Phase 1 expansion — 24 clubs across GB1, ES1, L1, IT1. Used by build:data-preview and merge pipeline.

stub.shortHistory: Chelsea grew from a west London club into a global name through Abramovich-era investment and later American ownership, with repeated Premier League and Champions League success since the 2000s.
stub.fanGuide: Blue is the colour; “Carefree” and Shed End culture matter to supporters. The rivalry with Arsenal and Spurs frames the London football map.
legends: Frank Lampard — midfielder and club-record goals
Didier Drogba — big-game striker
John Terry — captain and defensive leader

stub.shortHistory: United built the Busby Babes story, Ferguson’s 26-year dynasty, and a global fanbase. Old Trafford and the 1999 treble remain reference points for English football history.
stub.fanGuide: Red shirts, Stretford End songs, and the Liverpool rivalry define the emotional calendar. “Glory Glory Man United” is the anthem newcomers hear first.
legends: Sir Bobby Charlton — Munich survivor and club icon
George Best — flair winger of the 1960s
Cristiano Ronaldo — two spells, goals and global fame
currentKeyPlayers: Bruno Fernandes — Portugal playmaker and captain

stub.shortHistory: Spurs were the first English club to win a major European trophy in the modern era (1963). The north London rivalry with Arsenal is the club’s heartbeat; recent decades mixed near-miss seasons with new stadium ambition.
stub.fanGuide: White shirts and navy trim; the north London derby is the fixture to learn first. “When the year ends in one, Spurs win something” is a famous supporter joke — know it before you repeat it.
legends: Jimmy Greaves — prolific goalscorer
Glenn Hoddle — elegant playmaker
Harry Kane — academy striker and club record scorer
currentKeyPlayers: Son Heung-min — forward and South Korea captain

stub.shortHistory: Newcastle carry a passionate regional identity — the Tyne-Wear derby with Sunderland is historic. Modern investment aimed to restore trophy challenges after years of turbulence.
stub.fanGuide: Black and white stripes; Gallowgate End leads the noise. Supporters describe the club as more than football for the north east — learn the Tyne-Wear derby name even if fixtures are less frequent.
legends: Alan Shearer — local hero and Premier League record scorer
Peter Beardsley — technical forward
Kevin Keegan — player and beloved manager

stub.shortHistory: Villa are one of England’s founding clubs and European Cup winners in 1982. Birmingham’s claret and blue identity runs deep through the West Midlands.
stub.fanGuide: Claret and blue halves; Holte End is famous for volume. The Second City derby vs Birmingham City is the local grudge to know.
legends: Peter Withe — scorer of the 1982 European Cup final goal
Paul McGrath — elegant defender despite knee issues
Jack Grealish — local academy talent

stub.shortHistory: Atlético built a reputation as the capital’s gritty alternative to Real’s glamour — Simeone’s era added titles through organisation, set pieces, and fan intensity.
stub.fanGuide: Red and white stripes; the Madrid derby splits the city. “El Cholo” chants reference long-running manager Diego Simeone.
legends: Luis Aragonés — player and title-winning coach
Fernando Torres — local academy hero
Diego Godín — defensive rock of title years
currentKeyPlayers: Antoine Griezmann — forward and creative hub

stub.shortHistory: Sevilla became Europe’s specialist in the Europa League, winning the competition repeatedly while the big two dominated La Liga headlines.
stub.fanGuide: White shirts with red accents; the Seville derby vs Betis is one of Spain’s fiercest local rivalries.
legends: Diego Maradona — brief but iconic spell
Iván Rakitić — midfield engine of trophy eras
Jesús Navas — one-club full-back

stub.shortHistory: Dortmund’s Yellow Wall and youth development made them Germany’s romantic alternative to Bayern — Klopp’s pressing team and a Champions League final run define modern memory.
stub.fanGuide: Yellow and black; the Südtribüne is Europe’s most famous standing terrace. Der Klassiker vs Bayern is the national headline.
legends: Marco Reus — one-club winger and captain
Mats Hummels — centre-back and academy graduate
Robert Lewandowski — goal machine before Bayern
currentKeyPlayers: Jadon Sancho — winger on loan spells

stub.shortHistory: Leverkusen ended decades of “Neverkusen” jokes with an unbeaten Bundesliga title in 2024 — a club backed by pharmaceutical roots in North Rhine-Westphalia.
stub.fanGuide: Red and black kits; smaller than Dortmund or Bayern but fiercely supported in the Rhineland.
legends: Bernd Schuster — midfield playmaker
Rudi Völler — striker and later sporting director
Michael Ballack — powerful midfielder

stub.shortHistory: Leipzig rose quickly from lower leagues with Red Bull backing, becoming a Champions League regular and a foil to traditional German club culture.
stub.fanGuide: White and red; controversial among some German fans but loud and organised in Saxony.
legends: Yussuf Poulsen — Denmark striker and early symbol
Dayot Upamecano — defender before Bayern move

stub.shortHistory: Frankfurt mixed Bundesliga survival with European silverware — the 2022 Europa League win underlined their cup-night pedigree.
stub.fanGuide: Black, red, and white; Commerzbank-Arena nights are famous for atmosphere in the Rhine-Main region.
legends: Jürgen Grabowski — 1960s winger and World Cup winner
Bernd Hölzenbein — tricky winger of the 1970s

stub.shortHistory: Juventus hold the record for Serie A titles — the Old Lady’s black and white stripes symbolise Italian dominance, scandal, and rebuilds across decades.
stub.fanGuide: Black and white stripes; the Derby d’Italia vs Inter is a national grudge. Turin’s Derby della Mole vs Torino is the local fixture.
legends: Alessandro Del Piero — one-club symbol and free-kick artist
Gianluigi Buffon — goalkeeper for a generation
Michel Platini — French playmaker of the 1980s

stub.shortHistory: Napoli’s story is southern pride and Maradona’s 1987 scudetto — modern titles under Spalletti brought the city celebrations back to the streets.
stub.fanGuide: Sky blue shirts; Curva B is volcanic. The club is a symbol of Naples beyond football — learn Maradona’s name first.
legends: Diego Maradona — icon of the 1987 title
Ciro Ferrara — local defender
Gonzalo Higuaín — striker of title races

stub.shortHistory: Roma carry the capital’s giallorossi identity — Francesco Totti’s one-club career defines modern fan memory alongside sporadic title challenges.
stub.fanGuide: Yellow and red; the Derby della Capitale vs Lazio splits Rome. Totti’s number 10 shirt is still sacred to Curva Sud.
legends: Francesco Totti — one-club captain and Serie A icon
Daniele De Rossi — local midfielder warrior
Gabriel Batistuta — Argentine striker

stub.shortHistory: Lazio’s sky blue comes from Greek heritage branding — the Rome derby and ultras culture make them one of Italy’s most polarising clubs.
stub.fanGuide: Light blue shirts; Curva Nord at the Olimpico. Derby vs Roma is the first fixture Roman fans explain.
legends: Paolo Maldini — brief youth spell before Milan
Alessandro Nesta — elegant defender
Ciro Immobile — modern goalscorer
